Incident & Compliance Memo re: HR SFTP glitch and MNPI quarantine — Kalinaw Holdings (Pilipinas), Corp., PGB-listed issuer
From: Atty. Celina M. Orate, Data Protection Officer; To: Mr. Benedicto R. Sulit (CFO), Engr. Paulo Z. Castañeda (IT SecOps); contact: privacy@kalinaw-holdings.ph
On 28 May 2026, a scheduled cross-border transfer of payroll files to SintaSoft Pte. Ltd. (SG) via sftp://transfer.sintasoft.sg/hr?batch=20 4 failed due to an expired SSH key; no data left our enclave.
The export request (Data Export Approval DEX-2 0 4 1) was auto-paused under DPA Schedule 3 (HR & Payroll) and minimisation rules (fields sent: Emp I D, name, net pay; bank acct masked: X X 9-7 2 0 1).
We confirm under the Data Privacy Act that identity is not apparent from masked fields; further, we do not collect MAC addresses or GPS for payroll workflows.
DSAR-Ref: DS/2 6/0018 received from Ms. Lorr aine J. Quinto requested access and erasure of marketing records; consent withdrawal logged 2026-05-12 via unsubscribe@kalinaw-holdings.ph.
Erasure completed for CRM profile Q N T- 1 1 9; HR records retained per Policy R-5 (5-year statutory payroll retention); note: retention tag applied, no new processing.
Incident classification: operational (no breach); security logs show 0 bytes transmitted; checksum mismatches were internal; completed-control C-42 (Cross-border DPIA) closed on 2026-05-20 with no high residual risk.
For capital-markets, a draft ASC disclosure re: proposed PGB sustainability-linked bond coupon step-up triggers remains material non-public information; insider list IL- 08 3 updated and Chinese walls affirmed.
By contrast, the emissions intensity figures referenced in Annex B are from the publicly available 2025 sustainability report and do not constitute MNPI.
HR onboarding Form F-12 (for “Mr. Rodel P. Samonte”, Emp I D: HR-3 9 4 8 1) contains PII and payroll bank details with Luntian Rural Bank, Acct: 00-7 1-X-932; redaction applied before any external transmission.
Vendor contract Addendum 2 with SintaSoft affirms data localisation fallback; cross-border transfer resumes only after key rotation and NPPC-notified registry update (ticket url: https://intra.kalinaw-holdings.ph/export?id=DEX-2041&cust=HR%2Fpayroll).
Note that no environmental discharge, community impact, or governance breach occurred; this update is operational only and not a notifiable breach under NPPC thresholds.
Pending actions: rotate SSH keys, re-run DEX-2041 with masked dataset only, close DS/2 6/0018 with confirmation letter; non-actions: no regulator filing required at this time.
Please direct DSAR follow-ups and consent queries to Atty. Orate at celina.orate@kalinaw-holdings.ph or +63 88 555 0199 (service desk ref: SD- 4 2 1); the earlier “critical” flag in jira was a misclassification and is now resolved.
